search_receipt.method: Bounded web discovery emphasizing official standards, official project documentation, and primary research papers; claims were limited to documented component capabilities.
queries: official W3C Web Annotation Data Model selectors fragments provenance standard
official JATS XML standard article structure scholarly publishing NISO
RO-Crate specification provenance research object official
academic paper automated long-form research report generation STORM Stanford PaperQA2 Manubot
Manubot official documentation citations provenance collaborative scholarly manuscript
STORM Stanford paper assisting writing Wikipedia-like articles primary paper official
Argdown official argument mapping syntax documentation AIF argument interchange format primary
nanopublication official guidelines assertion provenance publication info trusty URI
search_receipt.selection_rule: Include primary or official sources that materially inform anchors, provenance, article interchange, argument representation, reproducible publishing, research automation, typed citations, or search receipts; exclude secondary summaries when a primary source is available.
limitations: This was a bounded landscape scan, not a systematic review, patent search, or proof of novelty.
Most URLs were registered from live primary pages without a local byte capture; their contents may change.
Failure to find a fully integrated comparable system does not prove none exists.

bounded_conclusion: The reviewed systems provide strong precedents for individual layers, but this bounded scan did not identify a single primary-source-described system combining revision-bound paragraph anchors, byte-exact evidence genealogy, typed objections and falsifiers, deterministic multi-version rendering, and fail-closed publication gates. This is an inference from the reviewed set, not a universal novelty claim.
